term: Specified trust beneficial interest (特定信託受益権)
plain_definition: Japan's trust-type stablecoin category, and one of the four statutory forms an 'electronic payment instrument' can take. It is a beneficial interest in a monetary trust, tokenized so it can be transferred electronically, where the trustee must keep a required minimum share of the money it holds in bank deposits and may invest the remainder only in government bonds and similarly safe, ordinance-specified instruments. A 2025 amendment relaxed those backing-asset rules, from 1 June 2026, by permitting reserve management via designated Japanese government bonds and early-withdrawal-permitted time deposits, subject to a composition-ratio ceiling and principal-protection conditions set at ordinance level.
formal_definition: Payment Services Act (資金決済に関する法律, Act No. 59 of 2009), Art. 2(9) -- confirmed by direct fetch of the current consolidated text via the e-Gov Law API this session (law_id 421AC0000000059) -- defines 'specified trust beneficial interest' (特定信託受益権) as a monetary trust beneficial interest, displayed as electronically transferable property value, where: the proportion of the total money received under the trust contract that the trustee manages as bank deposits meets an ordinance-specified minimum ratio; the remaining money is invested only by holding ordinance-specified government bonds or other ordinance-specified bonds (including book-entry transfer bonds under the Act on Book-Entry Transfer of Corporate Bonds, Shares, etc.); and other ordinance-specified requirements are met. It is item 3 of the four items comprising 'electronic payment instrument' under Art. 2(5). The backing-asset ratio and instrument rules were relaxed by 資金決済に関する法律の一部を改正する法律 (令和7年法律第66号, Act No. 66 of 2025), promulgated 13 June 2025, implementing ordinances promulgated 22 May 2026, effective 1 June 2026 -- per FSA's own summary, permitting reserve management via designated Japanese government bonds and early-withdrawal-permitted time deposits, subject to a composition-ratio ceiling and principal-protection conditions. The exact numeric ceiling reported in secondary commentary (approximately 50% of issuance amount) could not be independently machine-extracted from the underlying FSA ordinance PDF this session and is not asserted here as verified fact.
